meaning: v.(使)服从,(使)顺从vt.提交,递交
examples: Job applicants have to submit a specimen of handwriting.
It is laid down that all candidates must submit three copies of their dissertation.
They are inviting talented but unheralded movie-makers to submit examples of their work.
Had France decided to submit it instead of Austria, we certainly would ve accepted it.
There is little risk, but it will get you used to hitting the submit button.
